Template for memory allocation functions with size checks for different SDK versions.

# Purpose
This code provides memory allocation functions for a specific application type, identified by the placeholder `${name}`. It includes functions to allocate memory (`_applib_type_malloc_${name}`), allocate zero-initialized memory (`_applib_type_zalloc_${name}`), and determine the size of the memory block (`_applib_type_size_${name}`). The code uses conditional compilation to include size checks and padding assertions for different SDK versions, ensuring that the memory size and padding conform to specified limits for versions 2.x and 3.x. The use of `_Static_assert` ensures that these constraints are checked at compile time.

The code also includes optional instrumentation for memory allocation, which records the link register (`lr`) value if `MALLOC_INSTRUMENTATION` is defined. This can be useful for debugging or profiling memory usage. The functions rely on helper functions `prv_malloc`, `prv_zalloc`, and `prv_find_size`, which are not defined in this file, indicating that this code is part of a larger system where these functions are implemented elsewhere. The use of placeholders like `${name}` suggests that this file is a template, intended to be customized for different application types.
